items.title: Amazon is offering the Segway E3 Pro e-scooter down at $530 right now + other models starting from $140
items.summary:
While Segway has extended its Spring Sale EV lineup through to April 5th, we’ve spotted some of its popular e-scooters dropping lower in price over at Amazon, like the E3 Pro Electric Scooter at $529.99 shipped, for example, which beats its direct website pricing by $70. This newer model is listed with a $700 MSRP direct from Segway, however, you can more often find it down at $600 over at Amazon, with the Big Spring Sale pricing being retained here for added savings. While we have seen it go as low as $500 in the past, that rate hasn’t been seen since Black Friday, with the $70 markdown from its going rate (and $170 off the MSRP) being the next-best price to score it at. Head below for more on it and the other models seeing greater Amazon deals.

items.title: Everything is iPhone now

The thing about the iPhone is that everyone knew it was going to be a big deal, and then it was an even bigger deal than that. Hell, it's still the biggest thing going. items.title: Bag this adjustable aluminum tripod for your iPhone, Pixel, or Galaxy smartphone down at $18
items.summary:
Lamicall Adjustable Alumium Smartphone Tripod

Buy for $18 (Reg. $20)

Clip the on-page 10% off coupon.

What we love

If you want to step up the quality of your photos and videos shot on a smartphone, stabilization can really help. The aluminum tripod is quite adjustable and even comes with a remote for snapping pictures when you’re in the frame.

HUANUO 64-inch L-Shaped Electric Standing Desk

Buy for $240 (Reg. $350)

What we love

While this may cost more than some of the super budget-friendly models we cover from time to time, there’s a lot going on here that could make it worth the extra cash to some. For starters, it boasts a ton of space, but you’ll also find drawers, a sleek two-tone design, and the list goes on.

items.title: This Montessori-inspired app for kids has released a lifetime subscription for only $60 (Reg. $250)
items.summary:
Screen time feels like a trap when every app for kids is loud, flashy, and built to keep them tapping. Pok Pok is a calmer option. This Montessori-inspired app for kids ages 2 to 8 is ad-free, works offline, and avoids the flashing rewards and constant prompts that make some apps hard to turn off. Right now, you can get a lifetime subscription for only $59.99 (reg. $250).

more…

items.title: Swap boring bulbs with four of these Matter-certified Edison units at $7 a pop (26% off)
items.summary:
Linkind Matter Edison Bulbs (4-pack)

Buy for $28 (Reg. $38)

What we love

Affordably add stylish Edison bulbs to your smart home while these are just $7 a pop. Since they’re Matter-certified, they slip right in to Apple Home, Alexa, Google Home, and various other systems with no additional apps needed. I’ve personally been using Linkind smart bulbs in my home for over a year now and have no complaints.

items.title: Gotrax’s Dolphin e-bike with a 30-mile range is a budget-friendly commuter down at a $467 low
items.summary:
Amazon is currently offering an ongoing Lightning deal on the Gotrax Dolphin 26-inch Electric Bike for $467.49 shipped in three different colorways that is continuing over from the previous Big Spring Sale event. Normally going for $550 in full these days, this particular model has only seen two previous falls to $495 in 2026, with the latter of those having dropped further to this rate five days into the previous savings event. Now, for the first time since Black Friday and Christmas sales, you can jump on one of your own with an $83 markdown off its full price for a much more budget-friendly commuting solution that doesn’t weigh too heavily on your wallet.
